{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "in-progress",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 40,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "CSST gas piping not bonded — missing electrical bonding per manufacturer instructions and code",
    "Unbonded CSST is susceptible to arc perforation from nearby lightning strikes, creating gas leak and fire/explosion hazard",
    "Non-compliant with IFGC and NFPA 54 bonding requirements for corrugated stainless steel tubing"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop work and do not pressurize or place gas piping system into service",
    "Install proper bonding jumper per CSST manufacturer instructions — typically #6 AWG copper conductor from CSST to electrical service grounding electrode system",
    "Verify bonding connection is to a clean metal surface and listed clamp/bonding fitting is used",
    "Inspect entire CSST run for additional bonding or installation defects before proceeding",
    "Pressure test system only after bonding is complete and verified"
  ],
  "rationale": "Unbonded CSST presents a high risk of lightning-induced arcing that can perforate the tubing wall and release gas, creating fire and explosion hazard. IFGC and NFPA 54 require CSST to be bonded to the electrical service grounding electrode system. Manufacturer instructions are equally prescriptive and must be followed. Work must fail until bonding is properly installed and verified.",
  "references": [
    "IFGC Section 310 — Electrical Bonding",
    "NFPA 54 (National Fuel Gas Code) Section 7.13 — Electrical Bonding and Grounding",
    "CSST manufacturer installation instructions — bonding requirements"
  ]
}
